codeReview.summary: Implementation matches the feature contract: Settings sidebar now supports editable Profile Name with trimmed non-empty validation, max-length enforcement (64), inline errors, and save/cancel UX; AppStateProvider persists updates through the encrypted profile save path and updates in-memory state so the renamed device survives lock/unlock and reload. Added tests cover UI validation/literal rendering (unicode/emoji/RTL/XSS payloads), mutator validation, IndexedDB round-trip persistence, and lock/unlock persistence.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The ui-screen-worker procedure requires a Playwright demo-gallery run in verification, but this handoff reports followedProcedure=true without any Playwright command. Either the skill should explicitly allow skipping Playwright for scoped UI edits with strong component coverage, or workers should mark a deviation when omitting it.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/ui-screen-worker/SKILL.md (Work Procedure step 6 includes `npx playwright test src/e2e/demo-gallery.spec.ts --project=desktop --workers 1`); /Users/plebdev/.factory/missions/b48100dd-0e6c-4a7c-90a3-f12e61d1c3c4/handoffs/2026-04-22T16-31-55-920Z__m5-device-name-persist__096a7bf4-7858-44e7-98a1-9176afdb3ecd.json (`verification.commandsRun` has tsc/vitest/agent-browser only, no Playwright, while `skillFeedback.followedProcedure` is true).

summary: Reviewed transcript skeleton and commit diff for m5-device-name-persist. Code changes satisfy expected behavior and validation IDs for name editing, validation safety, and persistence across lock/unlock/reload; no blocking defects found. Noted one shared-state/process gap: procedure compliance reporting around required Playwright verification.
